title: Mecranium integrifolium
intro: Mecranium integrifolium, also known as "Pink Iresine," is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Melastomataceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Mecranium integrifolium include alkaloids, glycosides, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Mecranium integrifolium is commonly used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Mecranium integrifolium is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. The family Melastomataceae is commonly known as the melastome or memecyclid family. This family is a part of the order Myrtales, which includes other flowering plants with similar characteristics. The order Myrtales is a part of the clade Rosids, which is a large group of flowering plants that also includes rose and apple families. The clade Rosids is part of the larger clade of flowering plants called the Angiosperms.
